title: Today's AI Brief: Asana Acquires No-Code Agent-Builder StackAI
summary: Asana acquires StackAI, and a look at AI psychosis in CEOs
imageAlt: Image from source article for Asana acquires no-code agent-builder StackAI

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to your AI Brief. I've got a couple of interesting stories to share with you today. First up, workflow management platform Asana has acquired no-code agent-builder StackAI for $75 million. This move is part of Asana's larger effort to position itself as an AI-native workplace platform. StackAI's founders, Tony Rosinol and Bernard Aceituno, will be joining Asana as part of the deal. I'm curious to see how this acquisition will enhance Asana's automation capabilities and what it means for the future of workflow management.

So, what does this mean? Asana is essentially bolstering its automation capabilities by bringing StackAI's no-code agent-builder technology in-house. This allows Asana to offer more streamlined and automated workflows to its users, which could be a major draw for businesses looking to increase efficiency. I think it's a strategic play to enhance Asana's AI capabilities and stay competitive in the market. It's also worth noting that Asana is not the only company making moves in the AI space, and it will be interesting to see how they stack up against other players.

Asana acquires no-code agent-builder StackAI is also worth a quick mention. My quick read is that today's set of stories is useful because it gives us texture, not a sermon. Each item has its own little job: one tells us what changed, another tells us what people are worried about, and another gives the day its mood.
